special_features_inventory.scope: Visual analysis of 40+ buildings from field photos + HCD facade photos
special_features_inventory.purpose: Comprehensive catalogue of every observed decorative and architectural feature, keyed to the generator's decorative_elements schema and create_* functions for parametric modelling.

ARCHES.description: Brick or stone arch heads above window and door openings. The generator handles these via create_voussoirs() and create_window_lintels(). Arch type is set per-building in window_type or decorative_elements.stone_voussoirs.

segmental_arch.description: Shallow curved arch with less than semicircular curvature. The most common arch type in pre-1904 Kensington buildings. Formed with tapered brick voussoirs radiating from a centre below the spring line.

buildings.notes: Multi-ring arch over engine bay ~3m span. Single-ring segmental over windows.

buildings.material: buff stone voussoirs with raised keystone
buildings.notes: Finest residential example — consistent arches across 6+ windows with prominent keystones.
Typical Bellevue Ave quality.

buildings.material: buff stone voussoirs with raised keystones
buildings.notes: Three matching arches across facade. Strong directional light in photo reveals voussoir projection.
Three arched porch openings forming an arcade.
Fine brick cutting visible. Part of elaborate Victorian facade.
Large segmental arches ~1.0-1.2m span. Multi-ring voussoirs.
Consistent across semi-detached pair.
Arched ground floor windows below bow bay.
Part of polychromatic scheme.

segmental_arch.blender_notes: create_voussoirs() generates individual voussoir blocks radiating from spring line. Set stone_voussoirs.colour_hex to match observed stone colour (typically #D4B896 buff or #E0D0B0 cream).

flat_jack_arch.description: Flat or very slightly cambered arch formed by brick soldiers or wedge-cut bricks above an opening. Common on simpler residential buildings and post-1900 construction.

buildings.notes: Simple flat arches, 3-4 courses deep.
Standard residential flat lintels.
Matching pair.
Simple flat lintels on single-storey cottages.
Simple flat lintels above storefront windows.
Standard residential.
Simple flat lintel.

flat_jack_arch.blender_notes: create_window_lintels() handles these. Default behaviour when no arch type specified.

pointed_gothic_arch.description: Pointed arch with two curved segments meeting at a peak. Rare in Kensington residential — indicates Gothic Revival influence.

buildings.notes: Tall narrow pointed arch in the gable peak — the only confirmed Gothic pointed arch on a residential building in the HCD.
Full Gothic church — pointed arches throughout. Stone buttresses, lancet windows. The primary Gothic building in the HCD.

pointed_gothic_arch.blender_notes: Not natively supported by create_voussoirs(). Requires custom geometry or modification of arch profile. Flag for manual Blender adjustment.

CORBELLING.description: Stepped brick courses projecting progressively outward from the wall face, typically at cornice level or on gable peaks. The generator handles this via create_corbelling().

buildings.notes: Elaborate multi-course corbelling on both the main cornice and the hose-drying tower. Deep shadow lines.
Stepped gable profile and deep eave corbelling.
Full-width corbelled cornice at eave line.
Both gable peaks and eave line.
Gothic gable has stepped corbelling at peak.
Subtler corbelling beneath bargeboard.
Small corbelling at gable apex.

CORBELLING.blender_notes: create_corbelling() generates stepped brick courses. Set cornice.projection_mm (total) and cornice.height_mm. Each course steps out by projection_mm / n_courses.

POLYCHROMATIC_BRICKWORK.description: Deliberate use of contrasting brick or stone colours to create decorative patterns on the facade. Includes horizontal belt courses, decorative panels, and contrasting window surrounds. NOT incidental colour variation from mixed-quality brick.

horizontal_belt_courses.description: Continuous horizontal bands of contrasting material (buff brick, stone, or cast stone) dividing the facade into layers at floor levels.

buildings.notes: Buff brick belt courses at every floor level + as string courses. Also buff pilasters and arch voussoirs. The most extensive polychromatic scheme in the HCD.
Stone belt courses at each floor level. Stone window surrounds create additional contrast.
Buff brick bands plus geometric decorative panels between floors — most elaborate decorative brick patterns on Bellevue.
White stone belt courses strongly divide facade. Stone also used for all window surrounds.
Buff brick banding visible between floors.
Stone belt courses between floors on ornate Victorian pair.

horizontal_belt_courses.blender_notes: create_string_courses() generates horizontal bands. Set string_courses.colour_hex to buff (#D4B896) or white (#E0D8C8). Multiple bands need multiple entries or a belt_courses array in decorative_elements.

diaper_diamond_pattern.description: Diamond-shaped motif formed by inserting contrasting-colour brick headers at regular intervals within the running bond field. Very rare in Kensington.

buildings.notes: Repeating diamond motif in contrasting brick. The ONLY confirmed diaper pattern in the HCD. A significant heritage feature that must be accurately modelled.

diaper_diamond_pattern.blender_notes: NOT supported by any existing create_* function. Requires custom UV mapping or a dedicated diaper texture overlay. Flag for special Blender material treatment. The diamond pattern repeat spacing can calibrate brick course height.

decorative_panels.description: Recessed or projecting brick panels between floors or in spandrel areas using contrasting colours or textures.

buildings.notes: Geometric brick patterns including possible diaper-like motifs in buff and red brick. Most elaborate residential decorative brickwork on lower Bellevue.
Recessed decorative brick panels creating shadow lines.
Polychromatic decorative panels visible between storeys.

decorative_panels.blender_notes: create_string_courses() can approximate simple panels. Complex patterns need custom geometry or texture mapping.

GABLE_FEATURES.description: Decorative features in and on peaked gables. Bay-and-gable is the dominant Kensington typology.

bargeboard.description: Decorative wooden boards along the rake (sloped edge) of a gable, often with fretwork/scrollwork/gingerbread patterns. The generator handles this via create_bargeboard().

buildings.notes: The most ornate bargeboard observed — deeply cut scrollwork patterns.
Matching pair of ornate bargeboards on symmetrical double gable.
Gothic-influenced bargeboard on pointed gable.
Ornate Victorian bargeboard.
Peaked gable with decorative bargeboard.
Simpler bargeboard but with applied timbers in gable (half-timbering effect).
Twin gables each with finials at the apex — cross-gable roof.
Painted bargeboard on institutional/daycare building.
Adjacent units with different bargeboard colours.
Row of bay-and-gable houses, each with bargeboard. Styles vary.
Bay-and-gable row with bargeboard.
Victorian Carpenter Gothic fretwork at porch entrance and gable.
Simple bargeboard on painted/stucco house.

bargeboard.prevalence: Very high — present on virtually all bay-and-gable buildings (estimated 60-70% of residential buildings in HCD)
bargeboard.blender_notes: create_bargeboard() generates decorative boards along gable rake edges. Set style (plain/scrollwork/fretwork). Width typically 200-300mm. The scrollwork/fretwork styles use procedural cutout patterns.

ornamental_shingles.description: Decorative wooden shingles in the gable area, often with fish-scale, diamond, or scalloped patterns. Common in Queen Anne Revival buildings.

buildings.notes: Queen Anne Revival — decorative shingle cladding in the gable creates visual contrast with brick below.
Combination of ornamental shingles and applied timber framing in gable.
Shingles visible in gable between bargeboard.
May be present but partially obscured.
Distinctive green patina — possibly copper or painted metal shingles. Unusual.

ornamental_shingles.blender_notes: create_gable_shingles() generates shingle cladding on gable walls. Set exposure_mm (typically 100-150mm) and colour_hex. Fish-scale pattern is procedural.

gable_window.description: Window set within the peaked gable, often with a distinctive arch or decorative surround.

buildings.type: tall narrow with pointed Gothic arch
buildings.notes: Unique Gothic pointed arch gable window — only one in the HCD.
Gable windows with matching arch heads in both units.
Narrow gable window with decorative surround.
Elaborate gable window with apparent stained glass elements.
Decorative window shape in gable — possibly trefoil.
Two narrow windows in each gable.
Window in peaked gable with elaborate brick surround.

gable_window.blender_notes: create_gable_walls() includes gable window placement. Set roof_detail.gable_window fields. Pointed arch type needs custom handling.

half_timbering.description: Applied decorative timber framing in the gable, creating a Tudor/Arts and Crafts visual effect. Not structural.

buildings.notes: Applied timbers in gable creating half-timbering pattern over shingle infill.
Half-timbering visible in gable area.
Some units show half-timbering effect.

half_timbering.blender_notes: Not directly supported. Could approximate with applied box geometry on gable wall or custom texture. Consider adding to create_gable_shingles() as a variant.

BAY_WINDOWS.description: Projecting window structures extending from the facade. The generator handles these via create_bay_window().

canted_bay.description: Three-sided bay with angled (usually 45-degree) side panels. The most common bay type in Kensington.

buildings.notes: Matching pair of canted bays at second floor.
Small ground-floor canted bay.
Multiple bay-and-gable houses with canted bays.
Canted bay on painted facade.

canted_bay.prevalence: Very high — present on nearly all bay-and-gable houses (estimated 50-60% of residential)

bow_curved_bay.description: Curved/rounded projecting bay. Much rarer than canted bays in Kensington.

buildings.notes: CURVED/BOW bay window at ground floor. Rare in Kensington. Arched windows within the curved bay.
Large curved glass bay/turret addition — appears to be a modern renovation with conical roof cap. Unusual.

bow_curved_bay.blender_notes: create_bay_window() supports canted type. Bow/curved bay needs type: 'bow' with arc geometry. Flag for custom modelling.

oriel_bay.description: Upper-floor projecting bay that does not extend to the ground — supported by corbels or brackets.

buildings.notes: Distinctive red-painted oriel projection at top floor with its own small hipped roof cap. A unique feature on Augusta Ave.

oriel_bay.blender_notes: create_bay_window() with floor parameter set to upper floor only. The oriel's bracket support and hipped cap need special geometry.

PILASTERS.description: Shallow vertical projections from the wall face resembling flat columns. Used to divide the facade into bays or frame openings.
PILASTERS.generator_field: Custom — not in standard decorative_elements. Related to create_quoins().

buildings.notes: Buff brick pilasters with decorative capitals framing the main engine bay.
Full-height pilasters dividing facade and framing building edges. Project ~half brick.
Pilasters with stone/cast stone capitals defining vertical bays.

PILASTERS.blender_notes: create_quoins() handles corner projections. Full pilasters (mid-facade vertical strips) need a dedicated create_pilasters() function or extension of create_quoins().

OCULUS_WINDOWS.description: Circular (round) windows, sometimes called 'bull's-eye' or 'oeil-de-boeuf' windows. Decorative feature found on more elaborate buildings.
OCULUS_WINDOWS.generator_field: Custom window type — not standard in windows_detail

buildings.notes: Multiple oculus windows on the hose-drying clock tower.
Decorative oculi set between rectangular windows. Creates rhythm variation.

OCULUS_WINDOWS.blender_notes: cut_windows() only handles rectangular openings. Oculus windows need a circular boolean cut + radiating surround geometry. Flag for custom Blender modelling.

PORCHES_AND_VERANDAHS.description: Covered entrance structures. The generator handles these via create_porch() and create_turned_posts().

full_width_verandah.description: Covered porch spanning the full width of the facade at ground level.

buildings.notes: Shared verandah with ornate turned columns between the two units.
Full-width verandah creating second-floor balcony.
Multiple houses with full-width covered porches.
Deep covered porch across full facade.
Shared verandah with decorative posts.
Ground floor storefront with porch roof creating second-floor balcony.

full_width_verandah.prevalence: Very high — standard feature on most Victorian residential buildings

arched_porch_arcade.description: Porch with arched openings forming an arcade. Distinctive feature of some Queen Anne houses.

buildings.notes: Three arched porch openings forming a ground-floor arcade. Queen Anne Revival style.

arched_porch_arcade.blender_notes: create_porch() + create_voussoirs() combination. Set ground_floor_arches entries.

carpenter_gothic_porch.description: Small entrance porch with elaborate fretwork/gingerbread detailing in a Gothic-influenced style.

buildings.notes: Small covered entrance porch with decorative fretwork brackets and gingerbread trim.

CHIMNEYS.description: Brick chimney stacks visible at roofline. The generator handles these via create_chimney() and create_chimney_caps().
CHIMNEYS.prevalence: Very high — visible on virtually all pre-1930 buildings. Typically 1-2 per residential building, located at party walls or at the ridge.

notable_examples.notes: Twin chimney stacks at party wall and end wall, matching brick.
Large chimney on the main hall.
Row of chimneys visible along the shared party wall line.

CHIMNEYS.blender_notes: create_chimney() places brick chimney at ridge or party wall. create_chimney_caps() adds the corbelled cap detail.

peaked_gable.description: Front-facing peaked gable — the dominant roof form in the bay-and-gable typology.
peaked_gable.prevalence: 60-70% of residential buildings. Typically combined with a front bay window.

cross_gable.description: Intersecting gable roof forms creating an L or T-shaped roofline.

buildings.notes: Twin peaked gables forming a cross-gable composition.

mansard.description: French Second Empire mansard roof with steep lower slope and dormer windows.

buildings.notes: Mansard with dark shingle covering and dormer. Suggests 1870s-1880s construction.
Mansard-style upper storey with arched dormer windows on both units.

arched_dormer.description: Dormer window with an arched (semi-circular) head.

buildings.notes: Arched dormer windows on mansard roof — semi-circular arch heads.

gable_dormer.description: Dormer with its own peaked gable.

buildings.notes: Gable dormer at attic level.
Dormers in mansard slope.

SOLDIER_COURSES.description: Horizontal course of bricks placed on end (vertically) rather than laid flat. Used decoratively as belt courses, above windows, or at cornice level.

buildings.notes: Buff brick soldier courses forming flat lintels and decorative bands.

SOLDIER_COURSES.blender_notes: create_string_courses() — would need a 'soldier' variant that rotates bricks 90 degrees. Currently generates flat projecting courses.

ROWLOCK_SILLS.description: Window sills formed by a course of bricks laid on edge (rowlock orientation), projecting slightly from the wall face.
ROWLOCK_SILLS.prevalence: Very high — observed on most brick buildings where stone sills are not present.

buildings.notes: Clear brick rowlock sills on all windows.
Rowlock sills.
Rowlock sills.
Rowlock sills on cottage windows.
Rowlock sills.
Rowlock sills between windows.
Rowlock sills.

ROWLOCK_SILLS.blender_notes: Default sill geometry in cut_windows(). Projecting brick sill approximately 30mm out and 65mm deep.

STONE_SILLS_AND_LINTELS.description: Window sills and lintels in contrasting stone or cast stone. More expensive than brick — indicates higher-quality construction.

buildings.notes: Stone sills projecting from brick face on all windows.
Projecting stone sills.
Stone sills.
White stone sills and full window surrounds.
Stone or cast stone sills.

STONE_SILLS_AND_LINTELS.blender_notes: Set stone_lintels.colour_hex and stone_lintels.present. The generator applies contrasting material to lintel and sill geometry.

STOREFRONTS.description: Commercial ground-floor shopfronts with large display windows, signage area, and separate entrance.

buildings.notes: Ground floor commercial with awning. Brick above.
Ground floor commercial storefront with bench and display.
Large storefront window at ground floor.
Commercial ground floor with shop windows.
Heavily signed/graffitied commercial. Peaked gable above.
Row of small commercial buildings with murals/art.

STOREFRONTS.prevalence: Common on Augusta Ave and Baldwin St commercial strips

STAINED_GLASS_AND_TRANSOMS.description: Decorative glazing including stained or leaded glass in transoms above doors or in upper window sashes.

buildings.notes: Apparent stained or decorative leaded glass visible in the gable window of left unit (24). Yellow/green tones suggest art glass.
Gothic church with likely stained glass in lancet windows.
Possible decorative glazing in upper tower windows.

STAINED_GLASS_AND_TRANSOMS.blender_notes: create_stained_glass_transoms() generates coloured glass panels above doors. For windows, modify glass material with colour tint.

TOWERS_AND_TURRETS.description: Vertical tower elements projecting above the main roofline.

buildings.notes: Tall square brick tower with clock face, corbelled cornice, oculus windows, and pyramidal cap. The tallest and most prominent structure in the HCD. Already modelled as multi-volume building.

BALCONIES.description: Projecting platforms at upper floor levels with railings.

buildings.notes: Full-width iron railing balcony at second floor.
Wooden balconies with railings at second floor.
Metal railing balcony.
Second-floor balcony with iron railing on Italianate flat-roof building.
Metal balcony on left unit (orange brick).

wooden_porch_balcony.description: Second-floor balcony formed by the roof of the ground-floor porch.

buildings.notes: Porch roof creates balcony at first-floor level.
Shared verandah roof creates balcony.
Multiple houses with porch-roof balconies.

QUOINS.description: Corner stones or brick projections at building corners, creating a decorative vertical accent. Typically in contrasting colour.

buildings.notes: Possible stone quoins at building corners — partially obscured by vegetation.
Buff brick corner treatments that function as quoins.

QUOINS.blender_notes: create_quoins() generates alternating projecting blocks at building corners.

PAINTED_BRICK.description: Buildings where original brick has been painted over, obscuring texture and colour. Important for the 3D model to render accurately as painted rather than bare brick.

buildings.notes: Left unit painted green, right unit exposed brick.
Both units appear painted red.
Painted to match original brick colour but too uniform for bare brick.
Entire building painted yellow ('House of Moses'). Brick form visible through paint.
Right unit entirely painted white.
Grey painted brick or stucco.
Painted or rendered cream over original brick.
Right unit painted, obscuring polychromatic brickwork visible on unpainted left unit (12).
May be buff brick rather than painted — lower saturation could be original.

PAINTED_BRICK.blender_notes: Set facade_material to 'paint' and facade_colour to the observed painted colour hex. create_painted_material() generates a flat painted surface.

structural_cracks.description: Visible structural cracking in masonry indicating settlement or movement.

buildings.location: side elevation, vertical crack roof to foundation
buildings.notes: Significant vertical crack has been repaired/patched but clearly visible. Contrasting mortar colour in repair. Document for heritage condition assessment.

buttresses.description: Projecting masonry structures providing lateral support to walls.

buildings.notes: Stone buttresses along the nave — standard Gothic church structural feature.

BRICK_TEXTURE_TYPES.description: Five distinct brick texture categories identified from close-up analysis, each requiring a different Blender material.

type_B_residential_pressed.buildings: Most residential buildings across all streets

type_D_salvage_mixed.use: Rear walls, alley walls, garages only

type_E_side_wall_seconds.use: Side walls not visible from street

efflorescence.description: White salt deposits on brick surface from moisture migration.

repointing.description: Visible mortar repair with contrasting colour/material.

spalling.description: Surface delamination of brick faces from freeze-thaw cycles.

most_decorated_buildings_ranked.features: polychromatic banding, gauged arches, corbelling, pilasters, oculus windows, soldier courses, tower, quoins
gauged arches, oculus windows, stone belt courses, corbelling, pilasters, storefronts
polychromatic banding, segmental arches with keystones, corbelling, double gable with bargeboard, bay windows, ornamental shingles
polychromatic banding, decorative panels, Gothic pointed arch, corbelling, bargeboard
corbelling, pilasters, gauged arches, peaked gable, bargeboard, decorative panels
elaborate bargeboard fretwork, stone voussoirs, polychromatic banding, ornamental shingles
polychromatic banding, decorative panels, stained glass, stone voussoirs, bargeboard
diaper diamond pattern (unique in HCD), segmental arches
segmental arches with raised keystones, stone sills, balcony, storefront
Gothic pointed arches, buttresses, tower/spire, stone construction

generator_gaps_identified: Diaper/diamond brick patterns — no create_* function exists
Bow/curved bay windows — create_bay_window() only supports canted
Oculus/round windows — cut_windows() only supports rectangular
Pointed Gothic arches — create_voussoirs() only supports segmental
Half-timbering in gables — no create_* function exists
Oriel (upper-floor-only) bay windows — create_bay_window() assumes ground-up
Soldier courses — create_string_courses() uses flat brick orientation only
